title: Science Class Almost Blows Up (Slight Exaggeration)

sentences.en: Wednesday. Science class today had four activities packed into one hour, and my brain is still trying to recover.
sentences.zh: 星期三。今天理化課一個小時裡塞了四個活動，我的腦子到現在還在努力恢復。

sentences.en: First, we did a simple chemical experiment: mixing two colorless liquids together to see what would happen.
sentences.zh: 第一項，我們做了一個簡單的化學實驗：把兩種無色的液體混合在一起，看看會發生什麼事。

sentences.en: The moment I stirred the mixture, it turned a bright, glowing blue, and half the class gasped like we had discovered magic.
sentences.zh: 我一攪拌那個混合物，它馬上變成一種鮮豔發亮的藍色，全班有一半的人倒吸一口氣，好像我們發現了魔法一樣。

sentences.en: "This is not magic," our teacher said, smiling. "This is cause and effect. One chemical causes a reaction, and the color change is the result."
sentences.zh: 「這不是魔法，」老師笑著說：「這是因果關係，一種化學物質造成反應，顏色改變就是結果。」

sentences.en: She explained that even something as small as temperature can be a factor that changes how fast the reaction happens.
sentences.zh: 她解釋，就連溫度這種小小的東西，都可能是影響反應速度快慢的一個因素。

sentences.en: Next, we moved on to a station about electric energy, where we built a tiny circuit using a battery, some wire, and a small electrical motor.
sentences.zh: 接著，我們移到電力能量的站，用電池、電線，還有一顆小小的電動馬達，組了一個小電路。

sentences.en: When I connected the wires correctly, the little engine spun to life, and I felt genuinely proud of myself for two whole seconds.
sentences.zh: 當我把電線接對的時候，那顆小小的引擎就轉了起來，我真的為自己感到驕傲了整整兩秒鐘。

sentences.en: "This tiny engine works the same basic way as much bigger machines," the teacher said. "Only the size and the technology are different."
sentences.zh: 「這顆小小的引擎，運作原理跟更大型的機器基本上一樣，」老師說：「只是大小跟科技不一樣而已。」

sentences.en: She reminded us that every machine, no matter how advanced its technology, still needs some kind of system to make it function properly.
sentences.zh: 她提醒我們，不管一台機器的科技多先進，它還是需要某種系統，才能正常運作。

sentences.en: Third came a quick review of the human body: cells, the tiny basic units of life, and organs, which are made of many cells working together.
sentences.zh: 第三個活動是快速複習人體：細胞，也就是生命最基本的小小單位，以及器官，由許多細胞一起合作組成。

sentences.en: "Scientists did not always know cells existed," she said. "Someone had to discover them first, using an early kind of microscope."
sentences.zh: 「科學家並不是一開始就知道細胞存在，」她說：「一定有人先用早期的顯微鏡發現了它們。」

sentences.en: That discovery, she explained, completely changed how doctors understood the human body and every organ inside it.
sentences.zh: 她解釋，那項發現徹底改變了醫生對人體，以及裡面每一個器官的理解方式。

sentences.en: Finally, our last station was about the environment: we had to collect data on how much paper our class throws away in one single day.
sentences.zh: 最後一站是關於環境：我們要蒐集數據，記錄我們班一天到底丟掉多少紙張。

sentences.en: William's class, apparently, is doing something similar, collecting environmental data on the whole school for a bigger project.
sentences.zh: 顯然 William 那個班級也在做類似的事，蒐集全校的環境數據，做一個更大的專案。

sentences.en: "Good data does not lie," the teacher said. "If we produce this much paper waste every day, we need a system to reduce it."
sentences.zh: 「好的數據不會說謊，」老師說：「如果我們每天製造這麼多紙張垃圾，我們就需要一套系統來減少它。」

sentences.en: Bernice suggested we create a small recycling station right outside our classroom, and the teacher actually seemed to like the idea.
sentences.zh: Bernice 提議我們在教室外面弄一個小小的回收站，老師似乎真的滿喜歡這個提議。

sentences.en: By the end of class, my notebook was full of chemical reactions, electric circuits, human cells, and recycling data, all crammed onto one page.
sentences.zh: 下課的時候，我的筆記本上塞滿了化學反應、電路、人體細胞跟回收數據，全都擠在同一頁上。

sentences.en: I told Bernice that today's class felt less like one subject and more like four different classes wearing a lab coat.
sentences.zh: 我跟 Bernice 說，今天這堂課感覺不像一門課，比較像四門不同的課穿著同一件實驗衣一起上課。

sentences.en: Lesson of the day: science somehow connects glowing blue liquid, tiny engines, human cells, and our classroom trash can, all in one single hour.
sentences.zh: 今日心得：科學不知怎麼地，能把發亮的藍色液體、小小的引擎、人體細胞，還有我們教室的垃圾桶，全部連結在同一個小時裡。

quiz.question: What causes the liquid mixture to turn bright blue?
options: A chemical reaction between the two liquids.
Stirring it with a metal spoon.

quiz.question: What do the students collect for the environmental station?
options: Data on how much paper their class throws away.

quiz.question: What does Bernice suggest after the data collection activity?
options: Creating a small recycling station outside the classroom.
